title: Present perfect: form and use
explanation:
Present perfect (A2)

have / has + past participle

  • Life experience: I have visited Paris.
  • Unfinished time: She has worked here since 2020.
  • Recent past with just: They have just arrived.

Regular past participle: -ed. Irregular: third column (go → gone).

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 1
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option to complete the sentences.

questions.text: I [*have never been|never was|am never] to Japan.
She [*has lived|lived|lives] here for five years.
They [*have just finished|just finished|just finish] their homework.
[*Have you ever tried|Did you ever try|Do you ever try] Indian food?
He [*hasn't seen|didn't see|doesn't see] the film yet.
We [*have known|knew|know] each other since school.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: I ____ my keys. I can't find them. | *have lost | lost | lose
questions.feedback: Past action with present result → have lost.

questions.text: She ____ three emails this morning. | *has sent | sent | sends
They ____ to London many times. | *have been | were | are
He ____ his room. It looks tidy. | *has cleaned | cleaned | cleans

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 3
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option to complete the sentences.

questions.text: I [*haven't finished|didn't finish|don't finish] the report yet.
[*Has she ever met|Did she ever meet|Does she ever meet] a famous person?
We [*have already eaten|already ate|already eat] — we're not hungry.
